description_long: This lantern features a tall, multi-faceted body with a pointed, conical roof reminiscent of Gothic architecture. The upper section is adorned with arched, window-like glass panels set into a metallic frame, while the lower section is more robust and octagonal, also featuring arched glass panes. A metal handle with a chain is attached to one side, suggesting it can be carried or hung. The surface is textured with a diamond pattern on the roof and a smoother, darker finish on the lower panels. Decorative metalwork, including a circular medallion and ornamental rivets, adds to its historic and ornate appearance. The lantern is likely designed to house a candle or oil lamp, with glass panels protecting the flame and allowing light to shine through.
description: A tall, ornate lantern with a pointed roof, arched glass panels, and decorative metalwork, featuring a side handle and chain for hanging or carrying.
description_view_0: The lantern is seen from an angle highlighting the handle and chain on the left, with arched glass panels and the pointed roof clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view centers on the decorative medallion and symmetrical metalwork on the front, with the pointed roof and glass panels in full view.
description_view_2: The handle and chain are more prominent on the right side, with the lantern's octagonal base and arched glass panels visible.
description_view_3: A straight-on view of the lantern's back, showing uninterrupted glass panels and the pointed roof without the handle or medallion.
